parts.answer: An answer showing the following steps: • equation (1) • balanced equation (1) accept incorrect formula e.g. CO2 accept correct word equation e.g. C H + 6O → 6CO + 6H O ignore ATP / ADP 1 mark if equation correct but not balanced (2) number Answer Additional guidance Mark • B is the correct answer A is not the correct answer as 0.67 is not the RQ value C is not the correct answer as 1.06 is not the RQ value D is not the correct answer as 1.42 is not the RQ value (1) number Answer Additional guidance Mark • calculation of energy in ATP (1) • calculation of percentage useable energy with answer given to three significant figures (1) (122 x 30.51) = 3722.22 kJ (122 ÷ 12244) = 0.009964 (3722.22 ÷ 12244) x 100 = 30.4% (0.009964 x 30.51) x 100 =30.4% Correct answer gains both marks (2) number Answer Additional guidance Mark A is not the correct answer the sodium channel is not closed in depolarisation B is not the correct answer the sodium channel is not closed in depolarisation C is not the correct answer as the membrane potential is not decreasing in depolarisation (1) number Answer Additional guidance Mark • sodium (ion) {channels / voltage-gated channels} close (1) • potassium ions move out of cell via (voltage gated) potassium channels (1) • the inside of the axon becomes negatively charged (compared with the outside) (1) accept (voltage-gated) potassium (ion) channels open reject contradictions e.g. potassium ion moving in through open channels {decrease in membrane potential / back to (below)-70mv} ignore back to resting potential (3) number Answer Additional guidance Mark • measurement of scale bar converted to μm (1) • calculation of magnification and answer given in standard form (1) 9.5mm = 9500 (μm) ACCEPT 9000 to 10000 (μm) (9500÷50) = 190 = 1.9 x 10 accept 1.8 x 10 to 2.0 x 10 accept 2 x 10 180 to 200 gets 1 mark correct answer gains full marks (2) number Answer Additional guidance Mark • light (microscope) (1) mark
